title: Efficacy of Some Fungicides Against Fusarium Moniliforme And Macrophomina Phaseolina
abstract:
Fusarium moniliforme Sheld. and
Macrophomina phaseolina (Tassi) Goid
are important soil-borne pathogens which
attack a large number of crop plants. In
maize, they are known to cause stalk rots
which take a heavy toll every year. Hitherto,
the control measures suggested for these
~oil-borne fungi included mostly improved
cultural practices. In recent years, several
industries have developed numerous soil
fungicides, a few of which have proved
economical in controlling diseases of certain
c!Ops in other countries. In the present
study, an attempt was made to test
the efficacy of some of the fungicides against
these fungi both in vitro and in soil...
